charity.s: $15M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.13% of revenue).
charity.focus: Conservation, wildlife habitat protection, outdoor education, youth programs, military and veterans support, disaster relief

wiki.industry: fishing, hunting, shooting, camping, outdoor recreation
wiki.extract: BPS Direct, LLC, doing business as Bass Pro Shops, is an American privately held sporting goods retailer that offers hunting, fishing, camping, and other related outdoor recreation equipment, marine manufacturing and sales, and outdoor resorts. The stores feature natural outdoor designs and decorations. Bass Pro Shops support conservation efforts, organizations that support the United States Armed Services and Veterans, and outdoor education and recreation for youth. The company's headquarters, original store, and the Wonders of Wildlife National Museum & Aquarium are located in Springfield, M

cases.caseName: Gibson v. Cabela's Bass Pro Shop, Inc.
Tapia v. Archery Trade Association, Inc.
Babst v. Archery Trade Association, Inc.

health.s: 5 CPSC recalls on record; sample hazard: the tent's stove jack ring can deteriorate when exposed to high temperatures fro.

sampleRecords.part_of_body: Back, including spine, spinal cord, unspecified
sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by rolling object or equipment-other than powered vehicle, n.e.c.
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was pushing carts into the chute area for customers to retrieve. The first of six carts struck the wall and caused the other carts to bounce back and strike the employee. The carts knocked the employee to the ground and she sustained a back injury.
An employee fell in a locker room and was found unconscious on the floor, having suffered an injury to the back of the head.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level 11 to 15 feet
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee fell 15 feet to the floor from the top of a steel rack and sustained injury to both feet and ankles.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level 11 to 15 feet
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee fell approximately 15 feet to the floor from metal racking and landed on their feet. The employee was hospitalized for feet and ankle injuries.

sampleRecords.event_description: Part of occupant s body caught between vehicle and other object in nonroadway transport incident
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was driving a powered industrial truck. As it turned, the employee's right foot was raked between the machine and a rack, causing a cut to the foot/heel.

sampleRecalls.title: Westfield Outdoor Recalls Tents Due to Injury Hazard; Sold Exclusively at Cabela's and Bass Pro Shops (Recall Alert)
sampleRecalls.description: The recall involves only the Cabela's Ultimate Alaknak Outfitter, the Instinct Outfitter and the Big Horn 6P tents supplied by Westfield Outdoor. The tents' sizes range from 10 feet by 10 feet to 13 feet by 27 feet and are sold in green. The name, model number, date code (HF MMDDYY) providing the place and date of man…
hazards: The tent's stove jack ring can deteriorate when exposed to high temperatures from contact with a stove pipe and create smoke inside the tent, posing a risk of …
injuries: Westfield has received three reports of the stove jack ring deteriorating due to heat and creating smoke, and one report of smoke inhalation and frostbite related to discontinuing the use of a wood s…

sampleRecalls.title: Cabela's Recalls Food Dehydrators Due to Fire Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Cabela's commercial grade 80-liter and 160-liter food dehydrators. The stainless steel dehydrators have model numbers 541549 on the 80-liter, and 541650 on the 160-liter. Model numbers are located on the back panel of the dehydrator.
hazards: Defective wiring can cause a build-up of heat, posing a fire hazard.
injuries: The firm has received nine reports of the product malfunctioning due to defective wiring. No injuries have been reported.

sampleRecalls.title: Cabela's Recalls Electronic Jerky Blaster Due to Fire Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Cabela's Electronic Jerky Blaster, which resembles a caulking gun. The product has a black handle, a gold-toned 15" tube that holds the meat with "Cabela's Power Jerky" written on it and interchangeable tip at the end. The hand-held, battery powered appliance is used for making jerky sticks or str…
hazards: The battery charger adapter can overcharge causing the Electronic Jerky Blaster's battery and battery charger adapter to overheat.
injuries: There has been one report of the battery and the battery charger overheating. There are no reports of injury.

sampleRecalls.title: Purr-Fection Stuffed Animals with Flashlights Recalled by MJC Due to Laceration Hazard, Sold Exclusively at Cabela's
sampleRecalls.description: The recalled Beamerzzz stuffed toys come with LED flashlights sewn into the left paw. The toys are 12 inches high and come in five animal types. The model number can be found on the hang tag on the left ear. Affected toys are marked with manufacturing code 111208-02 on the sewn in label located in the left rear portio…
hazards: LED flashlight wires can protrude through the stuffed toy, posing a laceration hazard.
injuries: One incident was reported involving a consumer who noticed an exposed wire. No injuries were reported.
products: Beamerzzz™ Stuffed Animals with LED Flashlight

sampleRecalls.title: Electric "Worm Getter" Worm Probe Recalled By Six Retailers; Electrocution Hazard Cited
sampleRecalls.description: June 1, 1993 Release # 93-075 PRODUCT: Approximately 83,000 electric WG6-S and WG8-L "Worm Getter" worm probes, manufactured by Handy Marketing Company. The probes are used by fishermen to shock fishing worms to the soil's surface. The probes have a single energized metal rod, enclosed in a white plastic, spring- acti…
